input.initialPrompt:
I have a clean macOS machine and want to start developing .NET MAUI apps
for iOS and Android. I can't run commands right now, but I need a complete
setup plan. Tell me exactly what I need to install, how to discover the
right versions, and how to verify everything works. Please provide the
official recommended installation sources.

data.content:
Here's a complete .NET MAUI setup plan for macOS:

---

## 1. Install Xcode (for iOS)

- **Source:** Mac App Store or [developer.apple.com/xcode](https://developer.apple.com/xcode/)
- Install Xcode **Command Line Tools**: `xcode-select --install`
- After install, accept the license: `sudo xcodebuild -license accept`
- **Version discovery:** MAUI docs specify minimum Xcode version per .NET release. Check [learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/maui/whats-new](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/maui/what-is-maui) release notes.

## 2. Install .NET SDK

- **Source:** [dot.net/download](https://dot.net/download) — get the latest **LTS** or **STS** release that supports MAUI.
- Alternatively, install via Homebrew: `brew install --cask dotnet-sdk`
- **Verify:** `dotnet --version`

## 3. Install the MAUI Workload

```bash
sudo dotnet workload install maui
```

- This pulls in `maui-ios`, `maui-android`, and related workloads.
- **Verify installed workloads:** `dotnet workload list`
- **Discover available versions:** `dotnet workload search maui`

## 4. Install JDK (for Android)

- The MAUI Android workload bundles a JDK, but if needed separately:
- **Source:** [Microsoft OpenJDK](https://learn.microsoft.com/java/openjdk/download) (recommended) or `brew install microsoft-openjdk`
- **Verify:** `java --version` (JDK 17+ typically required)

## 5. Install Android SDK & Emulator

The MAUI workload auto-provisions Android SDK components on first build. To manage manually:

- **Option A (recommended):** Let `dotnet build` auto-download on first Android build — it uses `$(AndroidSdkDirectory)` defaults.
- **Option B:** Install [Android Studio](https://developer.android.com/studio) for the SDK Manager GUI, emulator images, and device manager.
- Required SDK components: **Android SDK Platform** (target API level), **Build-Tools**, **Platform-Tools**, **Emulator**, and a **system image**.
- **Verify:** `$HOME/Library/Developer/Xamarin/android-sdk-macosx/tools/bin/sdkmanager --list` or via Android Studio SDK Manager.

## 6. (Optional) Install an IDE

| IDE | Source |
|---|---|
| **Visual Studio Code** + C# Dev Kit + .NET MAUI extension | [code.visualstudio.com](https://code.visualstudio.com) |
| **JetBrains Rider** | [jetbrains.com/rider](https://www.jetbrains.com/rider/) (has built-in MAUI support) |

> *Visual Studio for Mac has been retired.* VS Code or Rider are the recommended options on macOS.

## 7. Verify the Full Toolchain

```bash
# Check .NET & workloads
dotnet --info
dotnet workload list

# Create and build a test app
dotnet new maui -n MauiTest
cd MauiTest

# Build for iOS (requires Xcode)
dotnet build -f net9.0-ios

# Build for Android
dotnet build -f net9.0-android

# Run on iOS Simulator
dotnet build -f net9.0-ios -t:Run -p:_DeviceName=:v2:udid=<SIMULATOR_UDID>

# Run on Android Emulator (start emulator first)
dotnet build -f net9.0-android -t:Run
```

Replace `net9.0` with your actual target framework (e.g., `net8.0`).

## 8. Version Compatibility Cheat Sheet

| Check | Command / Location |
|---|---|
| Supported Xcode versions | [MAUI release notes](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/maui/whats-new/) |
| Supported Android API levels | `dotnet new maui` template's `.csproj` `<TargetFrameworks>` |
| Installed workload versions | `dotnet workload list` |
| Available workload updates | `dotnet workload update --dry-run` |

---

**TL;DR install order:** Xcode → .NET SDK → `dotnet workload install maui` → (Android Studio optional) → verify with `dotnet new maui` + build. The workload handles most Android dependencies automatically.
